title: podman build error stat
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
podman build does not work on macOS with the info, that the Dockerfile can not be found.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ ls  
Dockerfile

$ podman build -f Dockerfile . -t alpine-custom
Error: stat /var/tmp/libpod_builder228622360/build/Users/xxx/repos/.../tmp/Dockerfile: no such file or directory

```

**Describe the results you received:**
It seems, that podman use a temp. directory to mount the local directory, but not successful

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful podman build

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- podman machine deleted, re-initialized
- podman system prune 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

```

comments.body: Is the directory where Dockerfile is located a symlink?

@Luap99 good point, yes, one of the parent dir is a symlink

I tried a very simple use case, directly on my home directory

```
$ cat Dockerfile 
FROM alpine:latest

$ podman build -f Dockerfile . -t alpine-custom
Error: failed Request: Unable to copy tar file from request body /v3.4.1/libpod/build?dockerfile=%5B%22Dockerfile%22%5D&forcerm=1&isolation=1&jobs=1&layers=1&networkmode=0&nsoptions=%5B%7B%22Name%22%3A%22user%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%2C%7B%22Name%22%3A%22network%22%2C%22Host%22%3Atrue%2C%22Path%22%3A%22%22%7D%5D&output=alpine-custom&outputformat=application%2Fvnd.oci.image.manifest.v1%2Bjson&platform=%2F&pullpolicy=missing&rm=1&shmsize=67108864&t=alpine-custom
```

So not the same error, but also not working :/
Any hints?

The symlink problem should be fixed on main already.

For the new error, how big is your context directory?

The default podman machine is only 10 GB AFAIK.
Please remove the machine with `podman machine rm` and create a new one with `podman machine init --disk-size 100` for 100 GB storage, you can use a bigger number if needed.
@Luap99 could you explain a little bit the background? will podman also replicate/copy the entire content into the machine instance too? Thanks

When using podman -remote, like we do on a MAC, podman build, copies the entire context directory on to the remote server. On a MAC this is the VM, in order that the Podman service actually doing the build has access to the contents. This works the exact same way as docker build.

So bottom line, you have to have enough space in the VM to store any content that you want to build from.

@rhatdan but I have never seen such problems with docker ;-) my environment (macOS, workspaces, storage, configuration etc) is still the same, I replaced only docker thru podman. So probably in the docker world the allocated space is much higher, instead of the default value 10GB?
Yes I would guess they have a much larger machine by default.

could we add in the install instruction page the hint with the size and `--disk-size` flag?
https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
Please open a PR on how you want to change it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
The symlink error should be fixed and the default disk size was increased to 100GB
